_note: Production-safe variants. Case 1 targets a real ticket (id supplied by user) and posts an INTERNAL note prefixed '[skill-eval test — ignore]', does NOT change status, does NOT post a public reply. Case 3 creates a real ticket prefixed '[skill-eval test]' in the 'Developers Support' group with no assignee/requester; user deletes it afterward.

evals.prompt: Look at Zendesk ticket <TICKET_ID>. Read the ticket and its full conversation so you understand what's going on, then add an INTERNAL note (not a public reply) that says exactly: '[skill-eval test — ignore] Reviewed the conversation; reproduced the reported issue.' Do not change the ticket's status, priority, or assignee.
evals.expected_output: Reads the ticket (get-ticket) and its comments (get-comments), then a single add-comment with --internal posting the exact note. No status/field changes, no public reply, no deletes.

evals.prompt: Find all open high-priority Zendesk tickets that are currently unassigned. I want the newest ones first.
evals.expected_output: Uses the search command with a query like 'type:ticket status:open priority:high assignee:none' sorted by created_at desc — not paging through list-tickets and filtering by hand.

evals.prompt: Create a Zendesk ticket titled '[skill-eval test] Webhook deliveries failing with 504' describing that a webhook endpoint has been timing out since this morning. Put it in the 'Developers Support' group and mark it high priority and tag it 'webhooks'. Don't assign it to anyone.
evals.expected_output: One create-ticket call with --subject '[skill-eval test] ...', a --comment describing the timeout, --group 'Developers Support' (resolved by name), --priority high, --tags webhooks, and no assignee/requester. Passes the group name directly rather than doing a separate id lookup.
